narrative:
Harry Kane is a Centre-Forward representing the United Kingdom. His overall rating stands at 7.72/10, positioning him in the top 1% of centre-forwards at this tournament. This rating has a range of 7.32–8.12, reflecting his current match sample size.

Kane demonstrates elite finishing, with 1.271 goals per 90 min at this World Cup against an expected goals of 0.67 per 90 min. His club form over the last two seasons also highlights this ability, scoring 1.219 goals per 90 min from an xG of 1.036. While primarily a scorer, his 0.763 key passes per 90 min at this World Cup show some creative contribution.

Kane serves as the primary goal threat and focal point for his national team, with 78% of his rating derived from consistent club form. Scouts should continue to observe his remarkable efficiency in front of goal, especially his ability to exceed expected goals. Fans should watch for his continued high volume of 3.559 shots per 90 min as the tournament progresses.
